_comment: Small(er) LLM kept resident alongside ComfyUI while it's actively generating -- activated automatically the moment a POST /comfyui/prompt request comes through the proxy (see handle_comfyui_proxy/activate_comfy_coexist in llama_priority_proxy.py), never manually needed. comfy_reserved_vram_bytes is subtracted from this scenario's own budget calc (on top of the usual headroom_gb and standalone VRAM) to cap how large this scenario's own LLM member is allowed to grow -- it does NOT reserve or enforce anything on ComfyUI itself (a separate process, unaware of this value entirely); it only throttles OUR sizing math. Set to 21GB: measured real ComfyUI usage for the current fp8 Flux.2 Klein workflow is ~20.3GB (see the VRAM-trace discussion this value comes from), and Qwen3.5-9B-UD-Q4_K_XL's own max_ctx=32768 cap (below) already keeps it at a fixed ~7.84GB regardless of this value AS LONG AS this stays under ~21.2GB -- so today this is a no-op, deliberately. It exists as a guard-rail: if this scenario's model or its max_ctx cap is ever changed later without re-checking the math, this stops it from silently growing enough to starve ComfyUI's real ~20.3GB need. Re-measure and adjust if the ComfyUI workflow itself changes (different checkpoint precision/size). fallback_for covers the scenarios that should keep answering chat requests, served by this same resident model, while this is active (see find_fallback) -- add more names here if more day-to-day scenarios exist later. IMPORTANT: this scenario's own 'priority' must stay HIGHER than every scenario named in fallback_for, or handle_completion's outranks_active check skips find_fallback entirely (going straight to a real, disruptive switch) instead of ever trying the fallback -- bump this if a future day-to-day scenario is added above priority 100. 'eager': true forces this member to spawn the instant the scenario activates (see eager_load_pinned_members) rather than waiting for a chat request to trigger the normal on-demand spawn -- without it, find_fallback would find nothing resident yet and every concurrent chat request would force a real (disruptive) scenario switch instead of the intended fallback. Qwen3.5-9B-UD-Q4_K_XL DOES have --mmproj (see its model_options.json entry), so unlike the qwen2.5-coder-1.5b it replaced here, a vision request during coexistence is now ALSO served by this same fallback instead of forcing a real switch -- the graceful-503 path below only applies to a scenario genuinely not covered by fallback_for. Its model_options.json max_ctx is capped to 32768 (well under its 262144 native ceiling) specifically for this coexistence duty, so 'auto' doesn't balloon it to ~16GB just because budget allows -- see benchmark_vram.py's max_ctx doc.
name: comfy_coexist
